market_read.summary: Large-cap AI leadership is firm, rates are the swing factor, and single-name earnings are creating sharper dispersion than the index tape suggests.
key_points: Policy and rates are the main macro constraint on risk appetite.
AI infrastructure demand remains a durable theme, but valuation discipline matters.
Several movers look catalyst-driven rather than broad beta-driven.

signals.title: AI capex beneficiaries still screen well
signals.summary: Cloud and chip commentary continues to point to elevated infrastructure demand, but power, memory, and networking constraints are the details to watch.

signals.title: Rates-sensitive software remains fragile
signals.summary: Long-duration names could struggle if yields continue to rise into upcoming inflation data.

movers.name: Example Semiconductors
movers.catalyst: Raised guidance and announced a new AI accelerator customer.
movers.interpretation: Move appears earnings-driven with a possible estimate-revision tailwind.

movers.name: Example Retail
movers.catalyst: Comparable sales missed consensus and management narrowed margin guidance.
movers.interpretation: The likely debate is whether the miss is macro-driven or company-specific.

opportunities.name: Memory cycle recovery
opportunities.setup: HBM demand and disciplined supply could support a stronger pricing cycle.
opportunities.mispricing: The market may still be underweighting operating leverage if AI memory demand proves durable.
bear_case: China or export controls pressure demand

watchlist.name: Inflation print
watchlist.why: Could reset rate expectations and long-duration equity multiples.

themes.name: AI infrastructure supply chain
themes.summary: The opportunity is moving from GPU scarcity into memory, networking, power, and cooling bottlenecks.
